title: Bolt.new Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using Bolt.new effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses to be low for more deterministic outputs.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4 optimized model with streaming for faster response times.
commands.scenario: When you need quick feedback on code during a live coding session.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Prompt pattern to encourage logical reasoning and verification in AI responses.
commands.scenario: When debugging complex algorithms and need thorough explanations.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control verbosity and cost.
commands.scenario: When generating concise code snippets or explanations.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting integration issues with Bolt.new.

commands.command: Use examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Prompt technique to improve AI understanding by providing examples.
commands.scenario: When the AI needs to generate code based on specific patterns.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API responses to ensure timely execution.
commands.scenario: When integrating Bolt.new in time-sensitive applications.

commands.description: Prompt technique to structure AI responses for better readability.
commands.scenario: When generating lists or step-by-step guides in code comments.

commands.description: Requests multiple completions to explore different solutions.
commands.scenario: When brainstorming multiple approaches to solve a coding problem.

commands.description: Simulates command execution without making actual changes.
commands.scenario: When testing CLI commands to ensure they perform as expected.

commands.command: Provide context about the problem domain.
commands.description: Prompt technique to improve AI's domain-specific responses.
commands.scenario: When working on niche or specialized software projects.

commands.description: Retries a failed request to handle transient errors.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in network-dependent applications.

commands.command: Implement a 'Plan-First' pattern: Request a detailed architecture outline and file structure before allowing any code generation.
commands.description: Prevents architectural drift by forcing the model to commit to a design pattern before writing implementation code.

commands.command: Use 'Incremental Refactoring' prompts: 'Refactor only the [specific function] to improve [specific metric] without changing the public API signature.'
commands.description: Reduces the risk of regression by limiting the scope of changes to specific logic blocks rather than entire files.

commands.command: Apply 'Constraint-Driven Development': Explicitly list forbidden libraries or patterns (e.g., 'Do not use Axios; use native Fetch API only').
commands.description: Ensures the generated code adheres to existing project standards and prevents dependency bloat.

commands.command: Debug via 'Error Trace Injection': Paste the full stack trace and ask: 'Analyze this trace, identify the root cause, and provide a fix that addresses the underlying state issue.'
commands.description: Moves beyond simple syntax fixes to address logical errors and state management bugs identified in logs.

commands.command: Optimize with 'Complexity Constraints': Append 'Ensure time complexity is O(n) or better' to algorithmic requests.
commands.description: Directs the model to prioritize efficient data structures and algorithms during the initial code generation phase.
